facts.text: South River Museum sits in the historic Old School Baptist Church, a building that has served the community for over 200 years.
In 1922 the church sold to the First Free Public Library for $5,000, and the library opened its doors in 1923.
The South River Historical & Preservation Society restored the church building with a grant from the Historic Preservation Bond Program, now hosting exhibits and meetings.
The library moved to a new building on Appleby Avenue in 1979, freeing the Old School Baptist Church for the society’s use.
South River’s borough council applied for the restoration grant after the building was badly in need of repairs, showing community investment in heritage.
The South River Museum showcases daily life, schools, churches, and local businesses, offering a window into the borough’s rich history.
